description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a sturdy metal frame. The panels are elevated above a flat base platform, which appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, and includes patterned and colored surface sections. The structure is supported by angled metal struts, providing stability and ensuring the solar panels are held at a consistent tilt. The base platform includes several compartments and raised sections, possibly for housing electrical components or providing access for maintenance. There are also visible ramps or access flaps on either end of the platform, suggesting points for entry or service. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for efficient solar energy capture and integration into urban environments.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array mounted on a metal frame above a patterned base platform with access ramps and compartments. The structure is robust and designed for efficient solar energy collection in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped solar panel array, patterned base, and a central access ramp.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled metal supports, the full length of the solar panel array, and the open access ramp on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the slope of the solar panels, rear platform details, and a visible label indicating the slope angle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the angled supports, the right-side access ramp, and the overall length of the structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 5 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth not clearly specified
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large panels forming a continuous rectangular array
